context: This final chapter of the Garuda Purana Saroddhara describes the vision of the Universal Form (Vishvarupa) of the Lord. It reveals the all-encompassing nature of the divine, presents final teachings on the unity of all existence within God, and concludes with the benediction and promise of liberation for those who hear and recite this sacred text.

verses.hindi:
गरुड उवाच —
विश्वरूपं तव देव दर्शयस्व ममाच्युत।
यत् ते रूपं परं दिव्यमप्रमेयं सनातनम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Garuḍa uvāca —
Viśvarūpaṁ tava deva darśayasva mamācyuta,
yat te rūpaṁ paraṁ divyam aprameyaṁ sanātanam.
verses.translation: Garuda said: O Lord Achyuta, show me Your Universal Form — that supreme, divine, immeasurable, and eternal form of Yours.

verses.hindi:
श्रीभगवानुवाच —
पश्य मे तार्क्ष्य रूपाणि शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः।
nानाविधानि दिव्यानि नानावर्णाकृतीनि च॥
verses.transliteration:
Śrī Bhagavān uvāca —
Paśya me Tārkṣya rūpāṇi śataśo'tha sahasraśaḥ,
nānāvidhāni divyāni nānāvarṇākṛtīni ca.
verses.translation: The Blessed Lord said: Behold, O Tarshkya, My forms by the hundreds and by the thousands — of many kinds, divine, of manifold colors and shapes.

verses.hindi:
इहैकस्थं जगत्कृत्स्नं पश्यादित्य सचराचरम्।
मम देहे गुडाकेश यच्चान्यद् द्रष्टुमिच्छसि॥
verses.transliteration:
Ihaika-sthaṁ jagat kṛtsnaṁ paśyāditya sacarācaram,
mama dehe Guḍākeśa yac cānyad draṣṭum icchasi.
verses.translation: Behold here, in one place, the entire universe — moving and unmoving — within My body, and whatever else you wish to see.

verses.hindi:
सूर्याचन्द्रमसौ नेत्रे द्यावापृथिव्यौ च मे मुखम्।
दिशः श्रोत्रे मनो बुद्धिर्वायुः प्राणो ममात्मकम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Sūryācandramasau netre dyāvāpṛthivyau ca me mukham,
diśaḥ śrotre mano buddhir vāyuḥ prāṇo mamātmakam.
verses.translation: The sun and moon are My eyes; heaven and earth are My face. The directions are My ears; the mind, intellect, wind, and life-breath are all of My nature.

verses.hindi:
सर्वतः पाणिपादं तत् सर्वतोऽक्षिशिरोमुखम्।
सर्वतः श्रुतिमल्लोके सर्वमावृत्य तिष्ठति॥
verses.transliteration:
Sarvataḥ pāṇipādaṁ tat sarvato'kṣiśiromukham,
sarvataḥ śrutimal loke sarvam āvṛtya tiṣṭhati.
verses.translation: With hands and feet everywhere, with eyes, heads, and mouths everywhere, with ears everywhere — He stands pervading all in the world.

verses.hindi:
मय्येव सकलं जातं मयि सर्वं प्रतिष्ठितम्।
मयि सर्वं च लीयन्ते तद् ब्रह्माद्वयमस्म्यहम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Mayy eva sakalaṁ jātaṁ mayi sarvaṁ pratiṣṭhitam,
mayi sarvaṁ ca līyante tad Brahmādvayam asmy aham.
verses.translation: In Me alone is everything born, in Me is everything established, and in Me does everything dissolve. I am that non-dual Brahman.

verses.hindi:
अनेकबाहूदरवक्त्रनेत्रं
पश्यामि त्वां सर्वतोऽनन्तरूपम्।
नान्तं न मध्यं न पुनस्तवादिं
पश्यामि विश्वेश्वर विश्वरूप॥
verses.transliteration:
Anekabāhūdaravaktranetraṁ
paśyāmi tvāṁ sarvato'nantarūpam,
nāntaṁ na madhyaṁ na punas tavādiṁ
paśyāmi Viśveśvara Viśvarūpa.
verses.translation: With many arms, stomachs, faces, and eyes, I see You everywhere in infinite form. I see no end, no middle, and no beginning of You, O Lord of the universe, O Universal Form.

verses.hindi:
त्वमक्षरं परमं वेदितव्यं
त्वमस्य विश्वस्य परं निधानम्।
त्वमव्ययः शाश्वतधर्मगोप्ता
सनातनस्त्वं पुरुषो मतो मे॥
verses.transliteration:
Tvam akṣaraṁ paramaṁ veditavyaṁ
tvam asya viśvasya paraṁ nidhānam,
tvam avyayaḥ śāśvatadharmagoptā
sanātanas tvaṁ Puruṣo mato me.
verses.translation: You are the imperishable, the supreme object of knowledge. You are the ultimate foundation of this universe. You are the unchanging guardian of the eternal dharma. You are the primeval Being — this is my conviction.

verses.hindi:
मत्तः परतरं नान्यत् किञ्चिदस्ति धनञ्जय।
मयि सर्वमिदं प्रोतं सूत्रे मणिगणा इव॥
verses.transliteration:
Mattaḥ parataraṁ nānyat kiñcid asti Dhanañjaya,
mayi sarvam idaṁ protaṁ sūtre maṇigaṇā iva.
verses.translation: There is nothing whatsoever higher than Me. All this is strung on Me, as clusters of gems on a thread.

verses.hindi:
अहं कृत्स्नस्य जगतः प्रभवः प्रलयस्तथा।
अहमादिश्च मध्यं च भूतानामन्त एव च॥
verses.transliteration:
Ahaṁ kṛtsnasya jagataḥ prabhavaḥ pralayas tathā,
aham ādiś ca madhyaṁ ca bhūtānām anta eva ca.
verses.translation: I am the origin and the dissolution of the entire universe. I am the beginning, the middle, and the end of all beings.

verses.hindi:
आदित्यानामहं विष्णुर्ज्योतिषां रविरंशुमान्।
नक्षत्राणामहं शशी वायुः प्रचलतामहम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Ādityānām ahaṁ Viṣṇur jyotiṣāṁ ravir aṁśumān,
nakṣatrāṇām ahaṁ Śaśī vāyuḥ pracalatām aham.
verses.translation: Among the Adityas I am Vishnu, among luminaries I am the radiant sun. Among the stars I am the moon, and among those that move I am the wind.

verses.hindi:
वेदानां सामवेदोऽस्मि देवानामस्मि वासवः।
इन्द्रियाणां मनश्चास्मि भूतानामस्मि चेतना॥
verses.transliteration:
Vedānāṁ Sāmavedo'smi devānām asmi Vāsavaḥ,
indriyāṇāṁ manaś cāsmi bhūtānām asmi cetanā.
verses.translation: Among the Vedas I am the Sama Veda; among the gods I am Indra. Among the senses I am the mind, and in living beings I am consciousness.

verses.hindi:
गङ्गा सरित्सु देवेषु ब्रह्मणो मम विभूतयः।
कालः कलयतामस्मि मृत्युः सर्वहरश्च अहम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Gaṅgā saritsu deveṣu Brahmaṇo mama vibhūtayaḥ,
kālaḥ kalayatām asmi mṛtyuḥ sarvaharaś ca aham.
verses.translation: Among rivers I am the Ganga; My divine manifestations are Brahma among the gods. Among reckoners I am Time, and among destroyers I am Death who takes all.

verses.hindi:
सर्वस्य चाहं हृदि सन्निविष्टो
मत्तः स्मृतिर्ज्ञानमपोहनं च।
वेदैश्च सर्वैरहमेव वेद्यो
वेदान्तकृद् वेदविदेव चाहम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Sarvasya cāhaṁ hṛdi sanniviṣṭo
mattaḥ smṛtir jñānam apohanaṁ ca,
vedaiś ca sarvair aham eva vedyo
vedāntakṛd vedavid eva cāham.
verses.translation: I am seated in the hearts of all beings. From Me come memory, knowledge, and their loss. I alone am to be known through all the Vedas. I am the author of the Vedanta, and I alone am the knower of the Vedas.

verses.hindi:
मामुपेत्य पुनर्जन्म दुःखालयमशाश्वतम्।
नाप्नुवन्ति महात्मानः संसिद्धिं परमां गताः॥
verses.transliteration:
Mām upetya punarjanma duḥkhālayam aśāśvatam,
nāpnuvanti mahātmānaḥ saṁsiddhiṁ paramāṁ gatāḥ.
verses.translation: Having reached Me, the great souls do not again take birth in this impermanent abode of suffering, for they have attained the highest perfection.

verses.hindi:
इदं ज्ञानमुपाश्रित्य मम साधर्म्यमागताः।
सर्गेऽपि नोपजायन्ते प्रलये न व्यथन्ति च॥
verses.transliteration:
Idaṁ jñānam upāśritya mama sādharmyam āgatāḥ,
sarge'pi nopajāyante pralaye na vyathanti ca.
verses.translation: Having taken refuge in this knowledge and attained My nature, they are not born again at the time of creation, nor do they suffer at the time of dissolution.

verses.hindi:
गरुड उवाच —
दृष्ट्वा ते विश्वरूपं मे भयं नष्टं महेश्वर।
प्रसीद देवदेवेश जगन्निवास नमोऽस्तु ते॥
verses.transliteration:
Garuḍa uvāca —
Dṛṣṭvā te viśvarūpaṁ me bhayaṁ naṣṭaṁ Maheśvara,
prasīda Devadeveśa jagannivāsa namo'stu te.
verses.translation: Garuda said: Having seen Your Universal Form, my fear is destroyed, O Great Lord. Be gracious, O Lord of the gods, O dwelling-place of the universe — salutations to You.

verses.hindi:
श्रीभगवानुवाच —
इदं गुह्यतमं शास्त्रं मयोक्तं ते खगेश्वर।
यः पठेत् श्रावयेद् वापि सर्वपापैः प्रमुच्यते॥
verses.transliteration:
Śrī Bhagavān uvāca —
Idaṁ guhyatamaṁ śāstraṁ mayoktaṁ te Khageśvara,
yaḥ paṭhet śrāvayed vāpi sarvapāpaiḥ pramucyate.
verses.translation: The Blessed Lord said: This most secret scripture has been told to you by Me, O Lord of birds. He who reads it or causes others to hear it is freed from all sins.

verses.hindi:
मरणे मरणोत्तरे च कृत्यं
यदुक्तं मम वक्त्रतो हि सर्वम्।
श्रवणात् पठनाच्चास्य नरः
प्रेतभयात् प्रमुच्यते॥
verses.transliteration:
Maraṇe maraṇottare ca kṛtyaṁ
yad uktaṁ mama vakrato hi sarvam,
śravaṇāt paṭhanāc cāsya naraḥ
pretabhayāt pramucyate.
verses.translation: All the rites at death and after death have been spoken from My mouth. By hearing and reading this, a person is freed from the fear of ghostly existence.

verses.hindi:
प्रेतत्वं नैव गच्छन्ति ये शृण्वन्ति इदं शुभम्।
गरुडोक्तं पुराणं हि पठ्यते मृतसन्निधौ॥
verses.transliteration:
Pretatvaṁ naiva gacchanti ye śṛṇvanti idaṁ śubham,
Garuḍoktaṁ purāṇaṁ hi paṭhyate mṛtasannidhau.
verses.translation: Those who hear this auspicious text never fall into the state of a preta (restless ghost). The Garuda Purana is therefore recited in the presence of the dead.

verses.hindi:
सद्गतिं लभते मर्त्यो यस्यान्तिके इदं पठेत्।
श्रावयेद् वा शृणोति यो मुक्तः संसारबन्धनात्॥
verses.transliteration:
Sadgatiṁ labhate martyo yasyāntike idaṁ paṭhet,
śrāvayed vā śṛṇoti yo muktaḥ saṁsārabandhanāt.
verses.translation: The mortal in whose presence this is recited attains a good destination. He who recites it for others or hears it is freed from the bondage of worldly existence.

verses.hindi:
षोडशाध्यायसंयुक्तं गरुडप्रोक्तमुत्तमम्।
सारोद्धारमिदं पुण्यं सर्वपापविनाशनम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Ṣoḍaśādhyāyasaṁyuktaṁ Garuḍaproktam uttamam,
Sāroddhāram idaṁ puṇyaṁ sarvapāpavināśanam.
verses.translation: This supreme text spoken to Garuda, comprising sixteen chapters, this holy Saroddhara — it is the destroyer of all sins.

verses.hindi:
य इदं पठति श्रद्धया नित्यं
गरुडप्रोक्तमखिलं सनातनम्।
स विमुक्तः सर्वपापेभ्यो
याति विष्णोः परं पदम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Ya idaṁ paṭhati śraddhayā nityaṁ
Garuḍaproktam akhilaṁ sanātanam,
sa vimuktaḥ sarvapāpebhyo
yāti Viṣṇoḥ paraṁ padam.
verses.translation: He who reads this entire eternal text spoken to Garuda with faith, regularly, is freed from all sins and goes to the supreme abode of Vishnu.

verses.hindi:
इति श्रीगरुडपुराणे सारोद्धारे विश्वरूपवर्णनं
नाम षोडशोऽध्यायः॥
इति श्रीगरुडपुराणसारोद्धारः सम्पूर्णः॥
verses.transliteration:
Iti Śrī Garuḍa Purāṇe Sāroddhāre Viśvarūpavarṇanaṁ
nāma ṣoḍaśo'dhyāyaḥ.
Iti Śrī Garuḍa Purāṇa Sāroddhāraḥ sampūrṇaḥ.
verses.translation: Thus ends the sixteenth chapter, entitled 'The Description of the Universal Form,' in the Saroddhara of the Shri Garuda Purana. Thus is the Saroddhara of the Shri Garuda Purana complete.
